flashcards.front: What is a function in discrete mathematics?
flashcards.back: A function f from set A (domain) to set B (codomain) is a relation where every element of A is associated with exactly one element of B. Denoted f: A → B.
A function f: A → B is one-to-one if different elements in domain map to different elements in codomain. If f(a₁) = f(a₂), then a₁ = a₂.
A function f: A → B is onto if every element in codomain B has at least one pre-image in domain A. Range of f equals codomain B.
A function that is both one-to-one and onto. Every element in domain maps to a unique element in codomain, and every codomain element is mapped.

flashcards.front: What is the Horizontal Line Test?
flashcards.back: A graphical test for one-to-one: If any horizontal line intersects the graph of a function at most once, the function is one-to-one.

flashcards.front: Define the Range of a function.
flashcards.back: The range (or image) of f: A → B is the set {f(a) : a ∈ A}. Always a subset of codomain B.

flashcards.front: What is the composition of two functions?
flashcards.back: Given f: A → B and g: B → C, the composition g∘f: A → C is defined as (g∘f)(a) = g(f(a)) for all a ∈ A.

flashcards.front: When does a function have an inverse?
flashcards.back: A function f: A → B has an inverse f⁻¹ if and only if f is bijective. Then f⁻¹: B → A satisfies f⁻¹(f(a)) = a and f(f⁻¹(b)) = b.
The identity function Iₐ: A → A maps every element to itself, i.e., Iₐ(a) = a for all a ∈ A. It is always bijective.
A function f: A → B where f(a) = c (fixed element) for all a ∈ A. Not one-to-one unless domain has only one element.

flashcards.front: If f and g are both one-to-one, is g∘f also one-to-one?
flashcards.back: Yes. If f: A → B and g: B → C are both one-to-one, then g∘f: A → C is also one-to-one.

flashcards.front: If f and g are both onto, is g∘f also onto?
flashcards.back: Yes. If f: A → B and g: B → C are both onto, then g∘f: A → C is also onto.

flashcards.front: What is the pigeonhole principle in functions?
flashcards.back: If |A| > |B|, no function f: A → B can be one-to-one. If |A| < |B|, no function f: A → B can be onto.
Floor ⌊x⌋ gives greatest integer ≤ x. Ceiling ⌈x⌉ gives smallest integer ≥ x. Both are functions from R to Z.
